title: On War
author: Carl von Clausewitz
content: “Everything in war is very simple, but the simplest thing is difficult. The difficulties accumulate and end by producing a kind of friction.”
action: Expect operational friction; build buffers to compensate.

title: The Infinite Game
author: Simon Sinek
content: “In a finite game, there are known players, fixed rules, and an agreed-upon objective. In an infinite game, players come and go, and the rules are changeable.”
action: Differentiate between finite tactics and infinite longevity.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
content: “A reliable way to make people believe in falsehoods is frequent repetition, because familiarity is not easily distinguished from truth.”
action: Control narrative loops to align perception with operational reality.

title: The Black Swan
author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb
content: “It is not about the visible but the hidden; we are not prepared for the unknown, we are only prepared for the expected.”
action: Design systems to be antifragile; leverage volatility.

title: Meditations
author: Marcus Aurelius
content: “The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts: therefore, guard accordingly.”
action: Maintain internal environmental integrity to protect external power.
